programme.title: Baby Driver
programme.desc: Coerced into working for a crime boss, a young getaway driver must face the music when a doomed heist threatens his life, love, and freedom.

programme.title: Constantine
programme.desc: John Constantine has literally been to Hell and back. When he teams up with a policewoman to solve the mysterious suicide of her twin sister, their investigation takes them through the world of demons and angels that exists beneath the landscape of contemporary Los Angeles.

programme.title: The Punisher
programme.desc: After Frank Castle's whole family is gunned down in cold blood, he shall become the vengeful Angel of Death known as the Great Equaliser of Crime: The Punisher. The one-man hurricane of blood and bullets is about to make the thugs who destroyed his life rue the day they made The Punisher come to life.

programme.title: Universal Soldier
programme.desc: An elite team of soldiers has been used against terrorists where they use astounding physical capabilities to overcome them. Victoria, a reporter, follows them and discovers a part of their secret. When one of the team kills her cameraman she tries to escape. Luc, one of the soldiers begins to have flashbacks and turns sides and helps her as the remainder of the team follows to protect their secrets.

programme.title: Mad Max
programme.desc: Taking place in a dystopian Australia in the near future, Mad Max tells the story of a highway patrolman cruising the squalid back roads that have become the breeding ground of criminals foraging for gasoline and scraps. After some grisly events at the hands of a motorcycle gang, Max sets out across the barren wastelands in search of revenge.

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Detective Sherlock Holmes and his stalwart partner Watson engage in a battle of wits and brawn with a nemesis whose plot is a threat to all of England.

programme.title: Total Recall
programme.desc: Douglas Quaid is haunted by a recurring dream about a journey to Mars. He hopes to find out more about this dream and buys a holiday at Rekall Inc. where they sell implanted memories. But something goes wrong with the memory implantation and he remembers being a secret agent fighting against the evil Mars administrator Cohaagen. Now the story really begins and it's a rollercoaster ride until the massive end of the movie.

programme.title: The Terminator
programme.desc: In the post-apocalyptic future, reigning tyrannical supercomputers teleport a cyborg assassin known as the "Terminator" back to 1984 to kill Sarah Connor, whose unborn son is destined to lead insurgents against 21st century mechanical hegemony. Meanwhile, the human-resistance movement dispatches a lone warrior to safeguard Sarah. Can he stop the virtually indestructible killing machine?

programme.title: Snatch
programme.desc: Unscrupulous boxing promoters, violent bookmakers, a Russian gangster, incompetent amateur robbers and supposedly Jewish jewelers fight to track down a priceless stolen diamond.

programme.title: Abraham Lincoln: Vampire Hunter
programme.desc: Abraham Lincoln, the 16th President of the United States, discovers vampires are planning to take over the United States. He makes it his mission to eliminate them.

programme.title: John Wick: Chapter 4
programme.desc: John Wick uncovers a path to defeating The High Table. But before he can earn his freedom, Wick must face off against a new enemy with powerful alliances across the globe and forces that turn old friends into foes.

programme.title: Guardians of the Galaxy
programme.desc: After stealing a mysterious orb in the far reaches of outer space, Peter Quill from Earth is now the main target of a manhunt led by the villain known as Ronan the Accuser. To help fight Ronan and his team and save the galaxy from his power, Quill creates a team of space heroes known as the "Guardians of the Galaxy" to save the galaxy.

programme.title: The Goonies
programme.desc: Young teen Mikey Walsh and his friends set off on a quest to find Pirate One-Eyed Willie's treasure in hopes of saving their homes from demolition. However, on their quest to find the treasure, they run into a family of recently escaped criminals, determined to capture the kids and reach the treasure first.

programme.title: Big Trouble in Little China
programme.desc: Truck driver Jack Burton gets embroiled in a supernatural battle when his best friend Wang Chi's green-eyed fiancée is kidnapped by henchmen of the sorcerer Lo Pan, who must marry a girl with green eyes in order to return to the human realm.

programme.title: Prince of Space
programme.desc: A Group of space men from the planet Krankor who resemble chickens are led by their leader, Phantom to invade Earth. But a strange superhero named Prince of Space (actually a bootblack in disguise) arrives to defeat the spacemen. Although the weapons of the aliens cannot harm the Prince of Space, the Phantom continues to fight, and many plot twists (including capturing a group of elderly scientists) ensue. Watch out for the Giant!

programme.title: Bride of Frankenstein
programme.desc: Baron Henry Frankenstein and his monster both turn out to be alive, not killed as previously believed. Frankenstein wants to get out of the evil-experiment business, but when mad scientist Dr. Pretorius kidnaps his wife, Frankenstein agrees to help him create a new creature, a woman, to be the companion of the monster.

programme.title: Creature from the Black Lagoon
programme.desc: A scientific expedition searching for fossils along the Amazon River discovers a prehistoric Gill-Man in the legendary Black Lagoon. The explorers capture the mysterious creature, but it breaks free. The Gill-Man returns to kidnap the lovely Kay, fiancée of one in the expedition, with whom he has fallen in love.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: Five grisly tales from a kid's comic book about a murdered father rising from his grave, a bizarre meteor, a vengeful husband, a mysterious crate's occupant, and a plague of cockroaches.

programme.title: Dracula
programme.desc: Transylvanian vampire Count Dracula bends a naive real estate agent to his will, then takes up residence at a London estate where he sleeps in his coffin by day and searches for potential victims by night.

programme.title: Ed Wood
programme.desc: Ambitious but troubled movie director Edward D. Wood Jr. tries his best to fulfill his dreams despite his lack of talent.

programme.title: Frankenstein
programme.desc: The 1931 horror movie "Frankenstein", tells the tale of when Henry Frankenstein who is a doctor, trying to discover a way to make a living human using human body parts from the recently deceased. He succeeds, but his creation is looked upon as a monster by the local inhabitants, who wish to destroy it.

programme.title: Frankenstein Meets the Spacemonster
programme.desc: When an atomic war on Mars destroys the planet's women, it's up to Martian Princess Marcuzan and her right-hand man Dr. Nadir to travel to earth and kidnap women for new breeding stock. Landing in Puerto Rico, they shoot down a NASA space capsule manned by an android. With his electronic brain damaged, the android terrorizes the island while the Martians raid beaches and pool parties.

programme.title: Frankenstein Meets the Wolf Man
programme.desc: The resurrected Wolf Man, seeking a cure for his malady, enlists the aid of a mad scientist, who claims he will not only rid the Wolf Man of his nocturnal metamorphosis, but also revive the frozen body of Frankenstein's inhuman creation.

programme.title: Hold That Ghost
programme.desc: Two bumbling service station attendants are left as the sole beneficiaries in a gangster's will. Their trip to claim their fortune is sidetracked when they are stranded in a haunted house along with several other strangers.

programme.title: House of Dracula
programme.desc: A scientist working on cures for rare afflictions, such as a bone softening agent made from molds to allow him to correct the spinal deformity of his nurse, finds the physical causes of lycanthropy in wolf-man Larry Talbot and of vampirism in Count Dracula, but himself becomes afflicted with homicidal madness while exchanging blood with Dracula.

programme.title: House of Frankenstein
programme.desc: After escaping from prison, the mad Dr. Gustav Niemann and his loyal hunchbacked assistant, Daniel, revive not only Count Dracula, but the Wolf Man and Frankenstein's Monster as well, all in order to exact revenge upon Niemann's many enemies.

programme.title: The Old Dark House
programme.desc: An American who sells cars in England receives a mysterious invitation from an old, eccentric millionaire to visit his house in which he lives with his twin brother.

programme.title: House of Wax
programme.desc: A sculptor opens a wax museum to showcase the likenesses of famous historical figures, but quickly runs into trouble when his business partner demands the exhibits become more extreme in order to increase profits.

programme.title: I Bury the Living
programme.desc: Through a series of macabre "coincidences," the newly-elected director of a cemetery (Richard Boone) begins to believe that he can cause the deaths of living owners of burial plots by merely changing the push-pin color from white (living) to black (dead) on a large wall map of the cemetery that notes those plots.

programme.title: Invaders from Mars
programme.desc: A young boy learns that space aliens are taking over the minds of earthlings.

programme.title: Invisible Agent
programme.desc: The Invisible Man's grandson uses his secret formula to spy on Nazi Germany.

programme.title: Island of Terror
programme.desc: A small, isolated island community's terrorised from a horde of creeping, blobbish, tentacled monsters which liquefy and digest the bones from living creatures. The community struggles to fight back, against these horrors.

programme.title: Tom Petty and the Heartbreakers - Rockpalast (1977)
Jethro Tull - Jack In The Green
ACDC - Live in Cleveland Full Concert (2003)
Ramones - Live At The Rainbow - December 31, 1977
Eric Clapton - Old Grey Whistle test (1977)
Columbo
programme.desc: One member of a mystery-writing-team decides to kill his more talented partner when the better writer decides to go solo.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: Through Vivian and Arthur, Maude and Walter meet new friends--who seem to like Maude and Walter more than they like Vivian and Arthur. It turns out that they are into wife-swapping and do not think Vivian and Arthur are the right partners.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: Maude stays home when the others go out to see a movie. When Walter, Vivian, and Arthur return, they walk into the house to find Walter & Maude's home was burglarized and Maude is tied up. Walter goes to extremes to find security.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: A full moon on Leap Year, prompts Blanche to throw a Moonlight Madness Party. However, her plans of being ogled by men go drastically awry when Dorothy gets all of the attention. Meanwhile, Rose contemplates marrying Miles after winning a free honeymoon to Paris.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: The full moon and festivities prompt strange happenings including Blanche's jewelry being swiped, Sophia trying to break a curse and Dorothy and Miles sharing a passionate kiss.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Miles' birthday is coming up, so Blanche convinces Rose to present him a naughty picture of herself as a gift. Meanwhile, Dorothy lands a highly-paid new job teaching adults, much to Sophia's delight.

programme.title: The Golden Palace
programme.desc: Rose organizes a romantic getaway night at the hotel with Dr. Ursula as a guest to give a lecture. Blanche is excited because Taylor Michaels is coming to town. She once lost this man to another woman but Rose is the one Taylor is interested in when he arrives.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Lamont purchases two coffins from an auction to try and get a profit if he sells them to a funeral parlor. In the process, Fred becomes paranoid by having the coffins in the house and refuses to sleep in the house with the coffins. Lamont doesn't think much of it, until he begins hearing strange noises at night.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Gloria is made to feel like an intellectual outcast when she spends the evening with Mike and his graduate-school friends.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.sub-title: The Best of All in the Family (1)
programme.desc: A look back at the first five seasons of all in the family with flashbacks, this episode was hosted by Henry Fonda.

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Rhonda & Kathy vs. Mark & Dianna vs. Samantha & Julie

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Mike & Roxann vs. Diane & Jody vs. Nancy & Rosemarie

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are sailors working in a ships' tailor shop. When they can't get passes to go ashore, they steal officers uniforms and go to a party with Curly passing himself off as Admiral Taylor and Moe and Larry as his aides. Two spies, one of them a beautiful woman, trick the stooges into stealing a new submarine. The boys turn the table on the spies and capture them. When the real Admiral shows up, Curly's reenacts the capture and accidentally detonates a bomb, blowing them all to kingdom come

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Larry, Moe and Curly have been inmates in a prison for ten years. Suddenly, they get a letter from their mama saying that their father has become rich and left her for another woman. The Stooges escape from the prison, intending to break up the wedding. What they end up finding out is that someone plans to get the drop on their old man. Curly, who happens to be a dead ringer for their father, is mistaken for him at the ceremony!

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are working as bellboys in a large hotel when a side show promoter shows up with 'Lupe', a wild wolfman who promptly escapes. The stooges try to capture the wolfman by playing music to calm him, but music makes the wolfman go berserk and soon the stooges are the ones trying to run away. The boys end up caught in an elevator with the wolfman who shoots them into the sky.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: When the stooges are left to do their income taxes together one day, they decide to get lots of money by defrauding the government with tax schemes. Once they are living richly they throw a party in which the main guest ends up being a government official who tells them the jig is up.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges escape from jail to prevent their newly rich dad from marrying Daisy, a gold-digger after his money. Shemp looks like dad, so he impersonates him and marries Daisy. What the stooges don't know is that Daisy is in cahoots with some crooks, and they plan to bump off her new husband. The boys foil the crook's plan and take Pa home to Ma.[ A remake of "Three Dumb Clucks", with Shemp, instead of Curly, playing both father and son. ]

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: After being accidentally dropped behind enemy lines, the Stooges disguise themselves and steal important plans from the Nazi high command.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The Stooges make what they think is a good swap â€“ their restaurant for a rundown race horse.

programme.title: Svengoolie
programme.desc: A scarred sculptor (Vincent Price) re-populates his ravaged wax museum with human corpses.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne's station wagon is hit by a luxury car that belongs to the rich Mrs. Wellman.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: On April 15th, Dan and Roseanne offer the kids a frantic lesson in family accounting; and in despair finally visit the local IRS office.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: In an attempt to bond with their daughters, Dan goes clothes shopping with Becky, and Roseanne watches basketball with Darlene.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne finally has a place to write when Dan builds her an office for her birthday -- but she's stricken with a case of writer's block.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Becky is eager to practice for her driving test, and the rest of the family awaits the results of Roseanne's pregnancy test.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Lending money to pal Arnie comes easily for Dan, but he believes that borrowing from family only adds up to disaster.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne decides it's time to give Becky "the talk", unaware that it's Darlene who needs it more.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Despite her misgivings, Roseanne, working late as a coffee-shop waitress, leaves Dan in charge of the kids.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: When Mr. Grace decides to open the store early, the staff set out to sabotage the new policy.

programme.title: Toon in With Me
programme.sub-title: Toony Thinks This is a Rerun
programme.desc:
Toony thinks he's seen this episode before, but Bill insists: This is not a rerun!
Featured cartoons:
Shutter Bugged Cat (1967) Hyde and Go Tweet (1960) Vim, Vigor and Vitaliky (1936) Pink Paradise (1967) A Hare Grows in Manhattan (1947)

programme.title: Columbo
programme.desc: Columbo matches his skills against Brimmer, a former cop turned private investigator with a quick temper who tries to blackmail a client's wife. When she refuses, he accidentally kills her and it's up to Columbo to nail him.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max runs into an old boyfriend, when she accompanies Caroline to see her father in jail.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline and Max spend their spring break dog-sitting at a couple's lavish apartment.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max convinces Caroline to partake in a clinical drug trial in an effort to raise money to help Caroline’s father.

programme.title: WWF Prime Time Wrestling
programme.desc: This special edition of Prime Time Wrestling emanates from the set of a western movie in Tucson, Arizona. While Bobby "The Brain" Heenan's antics disrupt the production, Gorilla Monsoon plots a little payback for his co-host. Plus, a look at The Mega Powers' appearance on The Brother Love Show, Tito Santana battles "Outlaw" Ron Bass, and more.

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: After the town's annual Lobsterfest is cancelled by a storm, Bob defiantly opens his doors for a festive celebration. When he wakes the next morning, Bob finds the restaurant in disarray and that the town has spurned him, but Bob won't let that deter him from further involvement in the celebratory weekend.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert proves to be a crashing bore when it comes to teaching traffic school, so he decides to practice his presentation on the Barone family, which drives them crazy. In an attempt to lighten things up, he puts in an assist-officer call to Traffic Cop Timmy, a puppet dressed as a policeman.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: As the entire family prepares for Honest Frank's Yard of Bargains, Ray is surprised to see Debra unloading their cribs and baby clothing for the sale, and even more surprised to hear that she assumed they were done having children.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Deacon and Kelly ask Doug and Carrie to be the godparents of their two children, Kirby and Major. Doug instantly says yes, while Carrie thinks this might not be such a good idea. Meanwhile, Arthur's boss has to go away for a few days and puts Arthur in charge of the pretzel shop.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Carrie leaves the garage door open and Doug's big screen TV is stolen.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Jerry's new couch is completely ruined when Elaine upsets a man with a bladder condition. Kramer decides to start up his own new pizza business. George joins a book club but instead of reading the book he watches the movie.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: George's girlfriend's mom thinks he is a bum when she catches George eating an éclair out of the trash can, among various other coincidences. Jerry dates a woman who is a former Olympic gymnast and ventures into the territory of sexual pleasures that most men dare not dream of. Elaine tries to pry Mr. Pitt away from looking at Kramer's new 3-D art poster. Meanwhile, Kramer gets a kidney stone.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne and Dan fear a financial crisis when they cannot sell the house they refurbished and their new partner, Roger, skips town.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Dan realizes that he needs a steady job to take care of his family when he accidentally knocks out one of Roseanne's teeth on the day she is to meet with a health inspector (who turns out to be Leon); Darlene is accepted to art school but David is rejected.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Michael comes to believe the office might be cursed in the wake of a freak accident. After scrutinizing each of his staffers' religious beliefs, he organizes a charity 5K "fun run."

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After meeting film star Rainier Wolfcastle at the gym ,Homer is volunteered by Bart to climb the treacherous local mountain the Murderhorn,as a publicity stunt for energy bars after Wolfcastle pulls out.Homer makes the climb despite warnings from Grampa and the exit of his sherpas and,at the top,makes a grisly discovery which helps his descent.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart’s latest stunt thwarts Lisa’s plan to attend a museum exhibit. When she tries to go alone and takes the wrong bus, Homer sets out to find her.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge and Homer discover that,by making love in public places, their sex life takes on an exciting new twist. They are almost caught in the nude at the novelty putting green, which leads to their having to make their escape in an air balloon which, unfortunately for them, having flown over a church with a glass roof with Homer hanging from the basket, comes to a stop in a crowded football stadium.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and Bart decide to get rich by selling cooking lard. At the same time Lisa is perturbed when Alex Whitney, a sophisticated new class-mate arrives at the school and becomes instantly popular. Lisa is tasked with organizing a school dance but after the lard has found its way onto school premises both Alex and Lisa have fun by throwing it at each other.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A midlife crisis prompts Homer to quit his job and become an inventor.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Against Al's objection, Peggy buys a home computer for the kids, to help them with their schoolwork, but it gets more use as a hat rack when no one wants to use it. Al begins to hear the computer talking to him and taunting him about his inferior life. At the same time, Al tries to train the not-so-bright Buck to fetch his bedroom slippers.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Seeking a tad more family togetherness (like TV's "Eight Family") and a family picture, Al declares "Bundy Day at the Beach", in which he takes the family to a local beach on Lake Michigan. Bud and Kelly compete with each other to find some dates for themselves, while an old flame, Marilyn Beamis, finds Al.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: When all the women in the neighborhood gets peeped on except Peggy, she gets really jealous and eventually puts out a ladder, milk, and cookies for the him. Meanwhile Al convinces Bud to stop fooling Kelly and actually help her with her studies.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: In the infamous "Lost Episode", Al and Peg learn they have been videotaped being intimate at a sleazy motel. The same thing has also happened to Steve and Marcy. They decide to take the matter to court.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: On Labor Day, the gender roles are reversed when Al becomes the loafer, while he forces Peggy to prepare the backyard barbecue for the creation of his famous "Bundy Burgers." But one of the secrets of their flavor is the ashes of Marcy's dead aunt, which Kelly stole from the Rhoades' house.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Peggy wins a two-week exercise course with local TV personality Jim Jupiter, "the healthiest man in Chicago," which soon turns into a test of wills between Peggy and the TV aerobics instructor. Jim succumbs to Peggy's lazy lifestyle of smoking and eating junk food. When Jim Jupiter literally drops dead afterward, during his TV show, Peggy surprisingly begins to suffer from a rare form of guilt.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Kelly and Bud need to raise money for concert tickets for themselves, so Bud sells Al as a camping guide, and Kelly cheats at a poker game with Peggy's friends. With Steve and Bud along for the ride, Al has to take a bunch of kids camping and they end up stranded in the woods and only Buck can save them.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: A Japanese land development company wants to buy out the neighborhood with plans to build a brewery in the area. Fred, holding out gets bitten by greed. Fred's greed is disguised as a "heartwarming" sentimentality and he's using it to squeeze as much money as he can get out of the developer.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.sub-title: My Kingdom For a Horse (a.k.a.) First Night Out
programme.desc: Fred makes a deal and buys a horse with a great racing history. Fred tries to get the animal to breed so that he can have a lot of money making champions hoofing and stomping around the place. However, there is just one problem Fred must overcome, the horse he bought is a gelding, which has Lamont saying "I told you so" to yet another one of Fred's moneymaking schemes.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred has a romantic night planned with Donna but Aunt Esther drops in at the last moment with some news, her neice, Elizabeth is in town and she wants Fred to have her stay with him. Fred is against the idea at first but when he takes one look at her he agrees, she is the spitting image of his late-wife, Elizabeth. However, there is another problem, Elizabeth is planning to move into an apartment in town and she will have two roommates, one female and the other male.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Julio's sister and nephew, Carlotta and Roberta, are coming for a visit and Julio has a problem, they are allergic to his pet goat, Chico. Thus, Julio asks Fred and Lamont if it would be okay for them to stay with them. Against the idea at first, Fred quickly softens to them and becomes involved with a problem that Roberto is dealing with at school.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: At the family reunion Chris is still struggling to get out from under the shadow of his cousin.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: Chris wins a weekend with his favorite talk show host, a contest the host first regrets he agreed to, but later Chris regrets he entered, when the host decides to stay.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Afraid of being late for work again Porky and Gabby try to sleep well during the night. But it doesn't work out so well for them.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Books and magazines come to life to sing and dance.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.sub-title: The Case of the Stuttering Pig
programme.desc: Porky, Petunia, and their brothers Peter, Patrick, Percy, and Portus, are set to inherit a fortune from their rich uncle. However the kindly Lawyer Goodwill, who is next in line for the fortune, drinks a bottle of Jekyll and Hyde juice and turns into a monster bent on killing Porky and his siblings.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: A take on Little Red Riding Hood.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Products in a store come to life as they sing and dance.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Daffy Duck torments Egghead.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: A travelling salesman is taken hostage by an African village.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Travelogue parody where we take a look at the land of Pingo Pongo.

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis sees the face of Jesus in a nacho chip, setting him on an epic journey to seek answers to the big question - "Why do I never score?"

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head go to the ""Creatures of Rock"" concert and spend the whole time waiting in line for the toilet.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Imitating a PBS documentary about Ben Franklin, Beavis and Butt-head get struck by lightning, and attract the interest of ""Betsy Wiener"" from ""Decency In Media.""

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys go to door-to-door to collect money for the poor as part of a class project; they meet up with a dominatrix.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys ditch school and work as temps at a real estate agency (and break stuff).

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-Head get stuck on top of Tom Anderson's house.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.sub-title: The Future of Beavis and Butt-head
programme.desc: The school's guidance counselor tries to help the boys figure out what their futures hold.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Butt-head orders a mail-order bride from Russia to ""get some;"" Beavis wants to watch.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis & Butt-head have to change the oil in the fryer; luckily, there's a gas station next door with plenty of oil... videos; Live - "I Alone" Ween - "I Can't Put My Finger On It" Rancid - "Nihilism"

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Two babes want the boys to change their flat tire, Butt-head learns what not to do with a tire-iron.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc:
"Buff-coat and Beaver" help Mr. Anderson build a swimming pool.
videos:
Sagat - "Why Is It? (Funk Dat)"
Ebn/Ozn - "AEIOU Sometimes Y"
Anthrax - "Hy Pro Glow"

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: Pee-wee leaves Mrs. Rene to house-sit the Playhouse while he goes camping with Cowboy Curtis. They ride horses, roast wieners and sleep under the stars. Pee-wee thinks Curtis is lucky to be a cowboy! Secret Word: SHOW

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: Pee-wee can't figure out what to "do" today, so Jambi gives him a to-do list which includes blowing a big balloon, dressing up as a pirate, and going for a walk with Miss Yvonne. The list also mentions cleaning up Pee-wee's room.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: After promising Earl to drop his tax return in the mail, Caroline learns that Max has never filed, and gets sidetracked trying to help her.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Richie meets with Tony to complain about his end of the garbage business, then meets with Junior to complain about Tony. Later, Tony orchestrates the 'planned bankruptcy' of David Scatino's sporting-goods store while Scatino's wife introduces Carmela to her handsome widowed brother.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Tony convinces Uncle Junior to seek a second opinion for his cancer treatment. Meanwhile, Carmela debates whether or not to take Dr. Melfi's advice and see a shrink, Big Pussy's wife Angie regrets asking Carmela for some money to help her sick dog, and Christopher continues to endure a humiliating probation period at the hands of kinky Paulie.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Newly-deceased Eleanor Shellstrop is sent to the Good Place, but only by mistake; Eleanor is determined to become a better person in her afterlife with help from friends Chidi and Janet; Eleanor tries to prove to Chidi that she's worthy of his help.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Chidi begins formally teaching Eleanor about ethics, and lesson No. 1 is: be kind to your neighbor. Michael and Janet help Chidi find a new, exciting hobby.

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: Rhaenyra continues her seemingly futile search for a suitable match, Daemon returns Westeros to Kings Landing and stirs more trouble for the King. Rhaenyra learns a valuable lesson about desires and what it means to be a daughter of a King.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: In King's Landing a grand meeting takes place. Theon makes amends and rescue plans. Sansa punishes those who wronged her family. Jaime confronts Cersei. The complete truth of Jon's lineage is revealed. The army of the dead reaches the Wall.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: A 15-year old faith healer collapses after laying his hands on an old woman in a walker. An MRI finds a brain abnormality, but he doesn't want it treated for fear that it might affect his healing capabilities. While in the hospital, the teen uses his power on a terminal patient Wilson is treating. Elsewhere, Wilson moves out of House's apartment, but he still wants in on House's weekly poker games.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: When a police officer with a gunshot wound to the head and uncontrollable laughter is admitted, House and the team are baffled. When Foreman begins showing the same symptoms, they race to determine the cause of the illness before Foreman's condition takes the same path.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: An anonymous letter writer makes ominous threats to Bart. The Simpsons discover they were sent by Sideshow Bob who, when released from prison, harasses the Simpsons and then attempts to kill Bart with a machete. But Bob's vanity foils him yet again.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer takes Nuclear Physics 101 at the local college, where he gets a trio of nerdy tutors expelled for extracurricular activities.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Burns' birthday is approaching and he longs for Bobo,the teddy bear he had as a child. Bobo has had an illustrious history,including flying the Atlantic with Lindbergh and being buried in Arctic ice,some of which is sold as ice cubes to the Simpsons so that Bobo is now Maggie's toy. Burns attempts theft,bribery and robbery to get his Bobo back but the family stand firm against him until finally Maggie,feeling sorry for him,offers him Bobo.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer, after seeing no more donuts in the power plant, says he would sell his soul for a donut, for the devil appears to be Ned Flanders. Whilst riding to school, Bart sees a gremlins on the side of the bus which only he can see. The Simpsons family go to Mr Burns' haunted castle where they think that Mr Burns is Count Dracula.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge starts hanging out with Ruth Powers from next door,to the annoyance of Homer,who feels neglected. When Chief Wiggum tries to flag the women down in Ruth's car Ruth keeps going,saying that she has stolen the car from her ex-husband. A high speed chase follows in the manner of 'Thelma and Louise',culminating at the top of a canyon,where the women manage to stop in time but Wiggum,with Homer as his passenger,goes flying into the void.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Springfield emulates Bart after he's praised by a self-help guru.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After a reckless Squishee bender with Milhouse, Bart wakes to the shocking realization that he joined the Junior Campers. Lured by pocketknives and knot-tying lessons, Bart assimilates into the group but then must face the father-son rafting trip.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer struggles with his attraction to new coworker Mindy, a beautiful engineer whose love for doughnuts is equal to his own.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: $pringfield (Or, How I Learned to Stop Worrying and Love Legalized Gambling)
programme.desc: Legalized gambling comes to Springfield, and Marge becomes addicted; Robert Goulet entertains at Bart's casino for kids.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer leads a vigilante group to catch the Springfield Cat Burglar, who then robs the museum.

programme.title: WWE Raw
programme.desc: Mr. McMahon crowns Kane with the WWE Championship. However, before the new champion can get used to wearing the gold, "Stone Cold" Steve Austin challenges him to a rematch in the main event. The Brawl for All Tournament begins, Ken Shamrock competes in a Triple Threat Match, and much more.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny and Moira struggle to find a place for their family portrait. David hosts a 'soft launch' of his new store.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny and Stevie celebrate having a fully booked hotel; David and Patrick go on a date.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Max reconnects with an old friend, Caroline’s jealousy inspires her to try to make some new friends of her own.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline sign up to be "booth babes" in order to get into a huge gaming convention for free. Once inside, they're shocked to see that Han's friend has created video game characters based on them, and demand compensation.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline shares her riches-to-rags life story on stage at a storytelling night and captures the attention of a Hollywood studio executive. Meanwhile, Sophie and Oleg search for a surrogate.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max tags along with Caroline as she travels to Los Angeles on a film studio’s dime to discuss the idea of making her riches-to-rags life story into a movie. While there, Max loses her luggage but finds a new crush.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline continues meeting with studio writers in an effort to make her life story into a movie, but she is torn when they reveal they would not portray Max in the project. Also, Max is being spoiled by her new Hollywood beau, Randy, who sets Caroline up on a date with his friend, Bob.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: As Max pet-sits Randy’s dog, Bruno, and Caroline sets up meetings with actresses who are interested in playing her in the potential biopic, Bruno escapes while a cannibalistic serial killer is loose in their neighborhood.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: The girls’ Hollywood adventure continues as Caroline signs away the rights to her life story so it can be turned a movie. Also, Max is heartbroken when Randy breaks up with her via his therapist, Elliot, and Caroline rents a party bus in an attempt to cheer her up.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The Show and Don’t Tell
programme.desc: When the girls go to prison to see Caroline’s father, Martin, perform in a musical, they tell him their plan to use Caroline’s movie money to convert their cupcake window into a dessert bar.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When the girls decide to look for a larger space for their dessert bar, Han sets them up with Evie, a real estate agent who has a crush on him. Also, Max is on the fence when Randy surprises her at the diner hoping to patch up their relationship, so she tells Caroline to decide for her.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The Attack of the Killer Apartment
programme.desc: In an attempt to get to know Max’s world better, her boyfriend Randy asks to spend a night at her apartment. Also, Caroline works on getting a liquor license for their dessert bar.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The Partnership Hits The Fan
programme.desc: When a law firm courting Randy for a job invites Randy and Max to dinner at a hip new restaurant, the evening veers off course when their eclectic cuisine doesn’t sit well with Max.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Max and Caroline need more space for their renovated dessert bar, they’re willing to do just about anything to convince Angie, the brash owner of the pizza place next door, to rent them her back office.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Max and Caroline discover that Han is in hot water after amassing substantial debt that he cannot repay from gambling on women’s tennis, the diner gang rallies to help him.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And the Two Openings: Part One & Part Two
programme.desc: As Max and Caroline, now part owners of the diner, put the finishing touches on their newly converted Dessert Bar, Max deals with the aftermath of her recent breakup with Randy, and Sophie and Oleg prepare for the birth of their baby.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline’s plans to attract a more sophisticated clientele to their dessert bar take a turn when Max befriends a bouncer whose team of arm wrestlers becomes their latest patrons. Also, Max and Randy continue to text each other, but when she runs out of sexy messages to send him, Oleg offers to be her ghost writer.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: An unexpected guest has Ted on edge. When Richmond travel to Manchester for a big match, Roy and Keeley become concerned about Jamie.

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.desc: - Light Sculpture - Paper Plate Illusion - Bacteria Sticker - Flash Fill - Ice Cubes Sticking - Childhood of Chimps - Word Processing - Pouring Along String

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.desc: - Giant Bubbles - Water Strider Sex I.D. - Banana Surprise - Hero The Robot - Snake Tracker - Uphill Funnel - Muscle Pairs - Wing Bee - Bottle Pickup

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: In this episode, Beakman shows how plants "eat," reveals a host of creepy animal facts and shows how to make and recycle paper.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman reveals how soap cleans and how explosions propel automobiles, and explores the question if birds sweat?

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman reveals the truth on electrical currents, if carrots improve eyesight and explores the workings of the electric light.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman tells us about sound and how explosions are made.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman explains how eyeglasses bend light, creates a needle compass and along the way discusses penguins.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: Magnetic fields; how to make a compass; why opposites attract.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: The relationship between the Earth, the sun, the wind and the weather. Guest: "Today" weather reporter Willard Scott.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: The Singing: The town of Elmore becomes an all-singing, all-dancing mash up. Everyone has a song inside them, but do they really need to be heard? / The Best: No matter what Gumball does, Carmen thinks she knows better. Gumball attempts to gain the moral high ground but fails spectacularly. He decides that self-improvement isn't his style. The only option he has left is to bring Carmen down to his level.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: The Wattersons compete over who has the worst life.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When Nicole finally gets a raise and an employee of the month plaque, Richard feels that he doesn't get enough credit for his work around the house. As a result, he goes on strike, causing the kids to revert to terrifying monsters.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When Leslie's petals begin falling off, he feels like his good looks are fading, so Gumball and Darwin use everything they know about style and beauty to try and help him.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Darwin is delighted when they discover their old childhood toys, but soon realizes that some things are best left in the past.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: The Wattersons fear the worst when they are summoned to a Town Hall meeting, but are surprised when they're offered a new house - until they discover it's in a different state.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: The Wattersons will do whatever it takes to get into the first screening of the latest Stellar Odyssey sequel.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When the kids find out that Mom's life hasn't panned out as she'd hoped, they start taking on her chores, but they accidentally grab her bucket list instead of her to-do list.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: From police chases to the inside scoop on Daisy the Donkey, Channel 6 Elmore News has all the top local stories. Experienced newscaster Kip Schlezinger brings the latest news, sports and weather, even when there is absolutely nothing to report.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When Anais is a newborn baby, Gumball and Darwin become jealous of their new sibling, and spark a baby war.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: Kicking a Ball and Pretending to Be Hurt
programme.desc: The Titans learn about the magic of soccer.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: When Beast Boy's head starts to rattle, Robin suggests that he should find a hobby so that his brain will stop shrinking.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Beast Boy and Cyborg decide to create a yearbook for the Titans.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Beast Boy discovers he can transform into an adult man.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Gizmo convinces the Titans he's kidnapped Cyborg, when in reality Cyborg's just hanging out in the HIVE tower with his girl Jinx. When Gizmo gets annoyed by Cyborg's constant presence, the two start a prank war.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Raven is cursed to be nice; meanwhile, Starfire is about to marry a pot of chili.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans are having a terrible time on their camping trip, so Robin suggests that they each tell a scary campfire story.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.sub-title: And the Award for Sound Design Goes to Rob
programme.desc: Raven just wants some piece and quiet for once, and accidentally makes a magical deal that eliminates all sounds.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The HIVE decides to take the day off so that they can avoid engaging with the Titans, if it is possible.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Beast Boy and Cyborg insist on hiring a clown for a Titans party, even though Robin tells them that clowns are for little kids. When the clown fails to live up to their memories, Cyborg and Beast Boy decide to give the clown some cool updates.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren is suffering from horrible allergies. Little does he know that he may be allergic to his best pal Stimpy.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren and Stimpy find an abandoned circus clown at their doorstep. While Ren DOES NOT want to keep him, Stimpy pleads and begs until Ren agrees.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren and Stimpy get jobs as bellhops and must make sure that no one finds out who is living in the top two floors of the hotel, but their curiosity gets the best of them.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Stimpy starts his journey into teenagedom, complete with all of its trials & tribulations.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren thinks TV has ruined Stimpy's mind, but when Stimpy wins a televised Gritty Kitty Litter contest and becomes a Hollywood star on the Muddy Muddskipper show, he leaves Ren behind.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis & Butt-head have to change the oil in the fryer; luckily, there's a gas station next door with plenty of oil... videos; Live - "I Alone" Ween - "I Can't Put My Finger On It" Rancid - "Nihilism"

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head visit a coffee house; Beavis downs gallons of cappuccino and takes the stage as Cornholio.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Lost in the woods, the boys hear a drumbeat and happen upon Van Driessen and his men's group -- having a monthly drum-banging session to let off steam as they ""wrestle with their manhood."" Beavis tries, and gets...a little carried away.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butthead kill another Friday night at the Maxi Mart. They try to service the girls by working the cash register and the manager, whose in the back room watching the camera, thinks that he's being robbed and calls the police.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The two boys cause huge chaos around Highland playing with a big tire they find at a garage.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Wild, Wild Kingdom will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Lisa gets made-up all flashy looking. When Alasdair insults her new look, she punches him, causing him to think he's different animals.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Carl Segan Meets Millions and Millions of Martians will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Doug goes into outer space to meet the aliens that he'd been communicating with.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Ripley's Incredibly Amazing Believe It or Not People will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross plugs his new egg farm (at Route 12, King Side Road) by having Alasdair break the world record for number of hard boiled eggs eaten in one sitting.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc: A big time Hollywood producer plans to make a major motion picture version of the show while the kids go through slime wrenching auditions just to get a part.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The A-Team Blows Up the Land of Oz will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast does many sketches about various fairy tales.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Bruce Springsteen Gets Laryngitis will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Alasdair believes that he will become a great rock star.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Mr. Wizard Explains the Universe will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Nerdy Matthew annoys the entire cast.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Mr. T Goes to a Tupperware Party will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast gets Ross to throw them a party. What Ross does, however, is have a cardboard cake and water.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Garbage of the Rich & Famous will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
After local garbage men go on strike, the studio becomes a storage area for the city's garbage. Christine is carried out of the studio in a garbage bag.

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Meat Handlers vs. Cartoon Voice Actors
programme.desc:
The rivalry between the cartoon voice actors and America's meat handlers is finally settled. Plus, Vic gives Kenny advice on being a TV announcer.
Games include: Sinkers and Floaters; Log Drop; Wall Buggers; Boulder Dash

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Those who take part in twelve step programs challenge those who gave a little bit of themselves in this edition of MXC, and the good captain talks about "tough love".
Games include: Mud Buttlers; Dash to Death; Rotating Surfboard of Death; Brass Balls; Pole Riders

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc: Dairy Workers vs. Automobile Workers (AKA Cows vs. Cars). Who will win out when its lactose versus lube jobs? Games: Wall Bangers, Get Hard!, Saddle Sores, Swish Bucklers, and the Teetering Temple of Crippling Doom.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
The good guys challenge the bad guys in this battle. Also, Kenny dresses up as a cop with an attitude and Vic gets into the mind of a criminal.
Games include: Dope on a Rope; Prison Break; Hand Job; Bunk Buddies; Legal Maze; Yankin' It

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Romance is in the air with a very special couples only show. Vic, Kenny, and the Captain discuss the ins and outs of love and relationships.
Games include: Plank Spankers; Romantic Rotating Surfboard of Death; Hot Steaming Bowl of Love; The Sticky Stuff of Love; Brass Balls of Love

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Washed-up child actors go head-to-head with the keen minds of the inventors on this show. Also, overhearing one ex-child actor's catch phrase, Vic and Kenny go crazy-go-nuts.
Games include: Get Over It; Sinkers and Floaters; Door Jam; Le Tour de Grand Prix; Log Drop

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Spring break means girls, girls and more girls on this special edition of MXC. Hot college chicks challenge hot college babes in a tournament to find out who's the best of the bunch. Vic and Kenny admire the ladies while the Captain gives some sex-ed advice.
Games include: Wall of Hidden Blistering Death; Blind-Sided Date; Sinkers and Floaters; Ball Busters; Log Drop

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Teachers challenge the rugged outdoors men and women on this edition of MXC. Guy also reports from his new medieval managed care facility, which is still surprisingly vacant.
Games include: Door Slammer; Sinkers and Floaters; Wall of Mame; Pole Riders; Log Drop

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria and Quinn have to take a road trip to bail Jane and Trent out of a traffic violation.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Secret service agents visit Lawndale High, and urge students to keep an eye of for students that might be different, and thus a threaten their safety, as well as the safety of the country. Though Daria & Jane become suspect numbers one and two, they actually start to notice an outbreak of weirdness among their fellow students, faculty members, and even relatives.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: The whole school goes on a mandatory-voluntary harbor cruise to raise money via casino gambling.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Jane starts dating a guy, to the annoyance of her misanthropic friend Daria.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: For an economics project, Daria and Jodie team up to start a company.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: While on a hill trek, Daria's class gets caught in an unexpected snowstorm.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: A football injury sidelines star quarterback Kevin, forcing him to re-evaluate his life for a minute or two.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: When a history test is stolen, Daria alone can solve the crime - provided she doesn't wake up.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback captures Colonel K and has the Colonel send Danger Mouse and Penfold on holiday to a haunted castle in Transylvania.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Baron Greenback has stolen Heinrich Von Squakenkluck's secret serum to create giant super duper chickens and has let one loose in the heart of London. Dangermouse and Penfold have to stop the Baron with a little help from Flying Officer Buggles Pigeon.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: The greatest evil genius the world has ever known, Baron Silas Greenback, plans to 'borrow, the hardest diamond in the world, "the Eye of Hercules". For with it attached it to his atomic powered mega drill, he can tunnel right into the bank of England. To make sure Dangermouse won't stop him, he has already dispatched his latest invention, the 'Martian Mouse exterminator' to get rid of DM.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Baron Silas Greenback has created a dream machine. The device resembles a colorful cloud. The dream machine descends upon DangerMouse and Penfold. They see no possible escape. Within the dream machine, whatever Penfold says is created. Terrified for a few minutes, DangerMouse and Penfold find a way to create just what they need to confront and defeat the villainous Greenback.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: 20.000 elephants have been turned into sugar-cubes by evil mastermind toad Silas Greenback. They only revert back to their normal size when dropped into a cup of tea. Dangermouse and Penfold travel to Africa, where an encounter with all the wild animals causes Penfold to revert into some bumbling kind of lord of the jungle.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: The world's top men are laughing themselves unconscious thanks to Baron GB's microscopic Gigelacockos germs that are spreading via telephone lines. Dangermouse, the white wonder and his trusty assistant Penfold race to save the British PM before the Baron and his crow henchmen can fire his Gigelacockos cannon all over London.
